commands.review_summary:
The PR reshapes WhatsApp `WebInboundMessage` into `event`, `payload`, `quote`, `group`, and `platform` contexts, preserves deprecated flat aliases, and updates WhatsApp runtime callers, docs, compatibility metadata, and tests.

PR surface: Source +679, Tests -23, Docs +31. Total +687 across 57 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a refactor/API migration PR rather than a reported bug. The behavior evidence is the PR’s focused tests, CI check-runs, and live WhatsApp scenario table.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Human maintainer review is the right lane because the PR is protected and changes a public WhatsApp callback contract plus broad message-delivery behavior; there is no narrow automated repair finding.

target.title: refactor(whatsapp): introduce inbound message contexts
target.body:
## Summary

This PR reorganizes WhatsApp `WebInboundMessage` so inbound callback data is grouped by what the data represents instead of being exposed only as one large flat object.

The new shape separates inbound message facts into five explicit contexts:

- `event`: message id, timestamp, and batching metadata
- `payload`: message body, media, location, and structured payload context
- `quote`: quoted/replied-to message context
- `group`: group subject, participants, and mention metadata
- `platform`: WhatsApp transport details and actions such as reply, media send, and typing

Downstream WhatsApp auto-reply, monitor, identity, group gating, reaction, reply delivery, media, quote, and test-support code now reads these facts from the new contexts. The shipped flat callback fields and methods remain available as deprecated aliases so existing callback consumers can upgrade without a breaking shape change.

## API Shape

Code that receives a WhatsApp inbound message should prefer the relevant context, for example `msg.payload.body`, `msg.event.id`, `msg.group?.mentions`, `msg.quote?.body`, and `msg.platform.reply(...)`.

For compatibility, existing flat callback fields and methods such as `msg.body`, `msg.chatId`, `msg.reply(...)`, and `msg.mediaPath` are still populated and marked deprecated in the exported `WebInboundMessage` type.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/whatsapp/src/monitor-inbox.behavior.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound.media.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`

## Live Scenario Validation

All recorded live scenarios are currently PASS.

| Scenario | Status | What was live-tested |
|---:|---|---|
| 01 | PASS | Public API exposes canonical nested inbound contexts and deprecated flat aliases. |
| 02 | PASS | Compatibility docs and registry describe the inbound context migration. |
| 03 | PASS | Normalizer accepts canonical/legacy input and rejects partial nested input. |
| 04 | PASS | Live `monitorWebInbox` direct text callback exposed nested contexts and aliases. |
| 05 | PASS | Direct DM auto-reply still replies after nested context migration. |
| 06 | PASS | Direct allowlist admits the configured sender. |
| 07 | PASS | Direct allowlist rejects an unconfigured sender. |
| 08 | PASS | Direct media inbound exposes `payload.media` and remains replyable. |
| 09 | PASS | Native WhatsApp location share exposed `payload.location`. |
| 10 | PASS | Native contact/vCard share exposed `payload.untrustedStructuredContext`. |
| 11 | PASS | Native quoted direct DM exposed canonical quote context and deprecated reply aliases. |
| 12 | PASS | Direct debounce batches rapid text messages with nested batch metadata. |
| 13 | PASS | Debounce exclusions behaved correctly. |
| 14 | PASS | `replyToMode: "first"` quotes the first inbound message. |
| 15 | PASS | Batched reply quote uses the selected source message id. |
| 16 | PASS | Group allowlist admits a configured group. |
| 17 | PASS | Group allowlist drops an unconfigured group. |
| 18 | PASS | Group sender allowlist admits the configured work sender. |
| 19 | PASS | Group sender allowlist blocks a non-allowed sender. |
| 20 | PASS | Explicit group mention triggers reply and sets mention context. |
| 21 | PASS | Unmentioned group message stores pending history and does not reply. |
| 22 | PASS | Later group mention injects pending group history. |
| 23 | PASS | Reply-to-bot in group acts as an implicit mention. |
| 24 | PASS | Group metadata context includes subject and participants when available. |
| 25 | PASS | Group context visibility filters pending history and quotes by sender policy. |
| 26 | PASS | Direct voice preflight preserves access control, ack timing, and transcript context. |
| 27 | PASS | Group voice transcript can satisfy mention gating after group policy passes. |
| 28 | PASS | Rejected group voice note does not transcribe or send feedback. |
| 29 | PASS | Broadcast group fanout routes configured agents with per-agent ack behavior. |
| 30 | PASS | Broadcast voice note shares one transcript while preserving per-agent route behavior. |
| 31 | PASS | Ack reactions target the exact inbound message ids and respect group mention state. |
| 32 | PASS | `reactionLevel: "off"` suppresses ack and status reactions. |
| 33 | PASS | Status reaction lifecycle handles visible delivery, silent handled hooks, and backend error delivery. |
| 34 | PASS | Text reply delivery uses `platform.reply`. |
| 35 | PASS | Media reply delivery uses `platform.sendMedia`. |
| 36 | PASS | `message_received` hook emits when enabled and is suppressed when disabled. |
| 37 | PASS | Multi-account override isolates policy, reply, reaction, debounce, and media config. |
| 38 | PASS | Direct peer identity and last-route updates use normalized nested sender facts. |
| 39 | PASS | Local media shape preserves media facts; remote-only URL variant is supported downstream but not producible by the live WhatsApp monitor path. |

## Review Note

The inbound callback contract now exposes the nested context shape while preserving the shipped flat callback aliases. The runtime only creates optional contexts when their facts exist, so non-media messages do not expose a truthy `payload.media` and direct debounced batches do not expose a truthy `group` context.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: WhatsApp inbound callback messages now expose event, payload, quote, group, and platform contexts while preserving deprecated flat aliases for the shipped callback contract.

Real environment tested: Local focused WhatsApp extension tests plus live WhatsApp scenario validation against real OpenClaw gateway/runtime and WhatsApp accounts.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: The commands listed in Verification were run after the final patch, and the live scenarios summarized below were exercised against real WhatsApp/OpenClaw runtime behavior.

Evidence after fix: Focused WhatsApp inbox and media tests passed, whitespace diff check passed, autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings, and all 39 recorded live scenarios are PASS.

Observed result after fix: Inbound monitor and auto-reply paths consume the nested message contexts for body, media, location, contact, quote, group, event, platform reply, media send, typing behavior, reactions, routing, hooks, and account overrides, while fallback flat aliases remain populated.

What was not tested: Broad Testbox/CI lanes. A few live variants required manual native WhatsApp input or source-runtime validation where the packaged/full-build path was impractical on this machine.
target.author: mcaxtr
